title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Redirects, Parser Limits, and Output Injection · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest site-quality team incrementally builds untrusted-input security boundary from original fixed response and HTML fixtures while retaining earlier authorization, HTTP, URL, provenance, politeness, accessibility, testing, security, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of untrusted-input security boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Allowlist schemes and destinations, reject credentials and dangerous address classes, constrain ports, and separate user input from fetch authority.
Resolve and classify every connection destination at the correct time, bind policy to the connected peer where possible, and recheck changes.
Apply full destination, credential, DNS, port, method, and budget policy on every redirect and browser subrequest.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ssrf Destination Policy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop site-quality team handles case 5577 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate SSRF destination policy, DNS and redirect checks, parser limits, and output injection defense; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-output-injection-defense-9gnbqg
# Competency: scraper-output-injection-defense.
# Case 5769: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_output_injection_defense_9gnbqg(candidate_destinations: int = 24, admitted_destinations: int = 14, rejected_destinations: int = 10, redirect_rechecks: int = 6, injection_findings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_9gnbqg = "9gnbqg-gqbng9-5769"
    assert evidence_variant_9gnbqg
    evidence_axes_9gnbqg = (
        "authorization9gnbqg",
        "url5769",
        "responsegqbng9",
        "tree9gnbqg5769",
        "frontier57699gnbqg",
        "reportgqbng95769",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_9gnbqg)) == 6
    if candidate_destinations < 1 or admitted_destinations < 0 or rejected_destinations < 0:
        return False, candidate_destinations, "security-count"
    if admitted_destinations + rejected_destinations != candidate_destinations:
        return False, admitted_destinations, "destination-reconciliation"
    if redirect_rechecks < 1:
        return False, redirect_rechecks, "redirect-revalidation"
    if injection_findings > 0:
        return False, injection_findings, "output-injection"
    assert admitted_destinations + rejected_destinations == candidate_destinations and injection_findings == 0
    return True, rejected_destinations, "secured-crawl-boundary"
